title: astragalus thracicus subsp. parnassi
intro: Astragalus thracicus subsp. parnassi is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to the Balkan Peninsula. It is also known as Parnassian milk-vetch, and its roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Astragalus thracicus subsp. parnassi include reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as supporting cardiovascular health and potentially reducing the risk of certain diseases. Astragalus thracicus subsp. parnassi is traditionally prepared as a tea or tincture, and it may also be found in capsule or powder form for use in herbal supplements.
